# to-words

> Convert numbers and currency amounts into words across 111 locales.
> Production-ready with BigInt, ordinal, TypeScript, ESM/CJS/UMD, and zero dependencies.

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/to-words
- GitHub: https://github.com/mastermunj/to-words
- Demo: https://mastermunj.github.io/to-words/
- License: MIT

## Installation

```bash
npm install to-words
```

## Quick Start

```js
// Class API
import { ToWords } from 'to-words';
const toWords = new ToWords({ localeCode: 'en-US' });
toWords.convert(12345);           // "Twelve Thousand Three Hundred Forty Five"
toWords.convert(1234.56, { currency: true });
// "One Thousand Two Hundred Thirty Four Dollars And Fifty Six Cents Only"
toWords.toOrdinal(21);            // "Twenty First"

// Functional API (full bundle — all 111 locales, ~58 KB gzip)
import { toWords, toOrdinal, toCurrency, toOrdinalSuffix } from 'to-words';
toWords(12345, { localeCode: 'en-US' });
toOrdinal(21, { localeCode: 'en-US' });
toCurrency(1234.56, { localeCode: 'en-IN' });
toOrdinalSuffix(3, { localeCode: 'en-US' }); // "3rd"

// Functional API — auto-detects runtime locale (navigator.language / Intl)
toWords(12345);
toCurrency(1234.56, { doNotAddOnly: true });

// Per-locale import — tree-shakeable, locale baked in (~3.5 KB gzip)
import { toWords, toOrdinal, toCurrency } from 'to-words/en-US';
toWords(12345);         // "Twelve Thousand Three Hundred Forty Five"
toOrdinal(21);          // "Twenty First"
toCurrency(1234.56);    // "One Thousand Two Hundred Thirty Four Dollars And Fifty Six Cents Only"
```

## Constructor Options

```typescript
new ToWords({
  localeCode?: string;           // BCP 47 locale code. Default: 'en-IN'
  converterOptions?: {
    currency?: boolean;          // Default: false
    ignoreDecimal?: boolean;     // Default: false
    ignoreZeroCurrency?: boolean;// Default: false — skip zero main unit
    doNotAddOnly?: boolean;      // Default: false — omit "Only" suffix
    currencyOptions?: {          // Override locale's default currency
      name: string;
      plural: string;
      symbol: string;
      fractionalUnit: { name: string; plural: string; symbol: string; };
    };
  };
})
```

## Class Methods

| Method | Signature | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `convert` | `(number: number \| bigint \| string, options?: ConverterOptions) => string` | Convert number to words |
| `toOrdinal` | `(number: number) => string` | Convert to ordinal words ("First", "Twenty Third") |
| `setLocale` | `(localeClass: ConstructorOf<LocaleInterface>) => this` | Inject a custom locale class |

## Functional Exports (full bundle: `import { … } from 'to-words'`)

| Export | Signature | Notes |
|--------|-----------|-------|
| `toWords` | `(n, opts?) => string` | `opts` accepts `localeCode`; omit to auto-detect |
| `toOrdinal` | `(n, opts?) => string` | `opts` accepts `localeCode` and `gender` |
| `toCurrency` | `(n, opts?) => string` | Shorthand for `convert(n, { currency: true })` |
| `toOrdinalSuffix` | `(n, opts?) => string` | Returns suffix only: "1st", "2nd", "3rd" |
| `detectLocale` | `(fallback?: string) => string` | Auto-detect locale from environment |
| `setLocaleDetector` | `(fn: (() => string) \| null) => void` | Override locale detection (server use) |
| `LOCALES` | `Record<string, ConstructorOf<LocaleInterface>>` | All bundled locale classes |

## Input Types

- `number` — standard JS number (safe up to `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`)
- `bigint` — for numbers beyond MAX_SAFE_INTEGER
- `string` — numeric string, parsed internally (avoids float precision issues for large numbers)

## Converter Options

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `currency` | boolean | false | Use currency format with locale units |
| `ignoreDecimal` | boolean | false | Ignore fractional part |
| `ignoreZeroCurrency` | boolean | false | Skip zero main currency |
| `doNotAddOnly` | boolean | false | Omit "Only" suffix in currency mode |
| `currencyOptions` | object | — | Override locale currency name/symbol |

## Custom Locale Injection

Any class implementing `LocaleInterface` (`{ config: LocaleConfig }`) can be passed to `setLocale()`. Copy the nearest built-in locale from `src/locales/` as a starting point.

```ts
import { ToWordsCore } from 'to-words';
import type { LocaleInterface, LocaleConfig } from 'to-words';

class BitcoinLocale implements LocaleInterface {
  config: LocaleConfig = {
    currency: {
      name: 'Bitcoin', plural: 'Bitcoins', symbol: '₿',
      fractionalUnit: { name: 'Satoshi', plural: 'Satoshis', symbol: 'sat' },
    },
    texts: { and: 'And', minus: 'Minus', only: 'Only', point: 'Point' },
    numberWordsMapping: [ /* same structure as any built-in locale */ ],
  };
}

const tw = new ToWordsCore();
tw.setLocale(BitcoinLocale);
tw.convert(2.1, { currency: true }); // "Two Bitcoins And Ten Satoshis Only"
```

## Auto Locale Detection

```js
import { detectLocale, setLocaleDetector } from 'to-words';

detectLocale();        // reads navigator.language (browser) or Intl (Node/Deno/Bun)
detectLocale('en-GB'); // custom fallback when detection fails

// Server: derive from request header
setLocaleDetector(() => req.headers['accept-language']?.split(',')[0] ?? 'en-US');
setLocaleDetector(null); // restore built-in detection
```

## CLI

```bash
npx to-words 12345
npx to-words 12345 --locale en-US
npx to-words 1234.56 --locale en-IN --currency
npx to-words 21 --locale en-US --ordinal
```

## Bundle Sizes

| Import | Raw | Gzip |
|--------|-----|------|
| Full bundle (all 111 locales) | 628 KB | 58 KB |
| Single locale (e.g. `to-words/en-US`) | 12 KB | 3.5 KB |

## Performance (Apple M2, Node.js 23)

| Operation | Throughput |
|-----------|------------|
| Small integers (42) | ~4.7M ops/sec |
| Medium integers (12,345) | ~2.2M ops/sec |
| Large integers (15 digits) | ~700K ops/sec |
| Currency conversion | ~1M ops/sec |
| BigInt (30+ digits) | ~225K ops/sec |

## Runtime Compatibility

Node.js 20+, Deno, Bun, Cloudflare Workers, all modern browsers (ESM + UMD).

## Supported Locales (111)

af-ZA, am-ET, ar-AE, ar-LB, ar-MA, ar-SA, az-AZ, be-BY, bg-BG, bn-IN,
ca-ES, cs-CZ, da-DK, de-AT, de-CH, de-DE, ee-EE, el-GR,
en-AE, en-AU, en-BD, en-CA, en-GB, en-GH, en-HK, en-IE, en-IN, en-JM,
en-KE, en-LK, en-MA, en-MM, en-MU, en-MY, en-NG, en-NP, en-NZ, en-OM,
en-PH, en-PK, en-SA, en-SG, en-TT, en-TZ, en-UG, en-US, en-ZA, en-ZW,
es-AR, es-CL, es-CO, es-ES, es-MX, es-US, es-VE,
fa-IR, fi-FI, fil-PH,
fr-BE, fr-CA, fr-FR, fr-MA, fr-SA,
gu-IN, ha-NG, hbo-IL, he-IL, hi-IN, hr-HR, hu-HU,
id-ID, is-IS, it-IT, ja-JP, ka-GE, kn-IN, ko-KR,
lt-LT, lv-LV, mr-IN, ms-MY, ms-SG, nb-NO, nl-NL, nl-SR, np-NP,
pa-IN, pl-PL, pt-AO, pt-BR, pt-MZ, pt-PT,
ro-RO, ru-RU, sk-SK, sl-SI, sq-AL, sr-RS, sv-SE,
sw-KE, sw-TZ, ta-IN, te-IN, th-TH, tr-TR,
uk-UA, ur-PK, vi-VN, yo-NG, zh-CN, zh-TW

## Common Examples

```js
// Basic
toWords(0, { localeCode: 'en-US' });           // "Zero"
toWords(-42, { localeCode: 'en-US' });          // "Minus Forty Two"
toWords(1000000, { localeCode: 'en-IN' });      // "Ten Lakh"
toWords(1000000, { localeCode: 'en-US' });      // "One Million"

// Currency
toCurrency(0.50, { localeCode: 'en-US' });
// "Zero Dollars And Fifty Cents Only"

toCurrency(1234.56, { localeCode: 'en-IN' });
// "One Thousand Two Hundred Thirty Four Rupees And Fifty Six Paise Only"

// Big numbers (use string input to avoid float precision issues)
toWords('123456789012345', { localeCode: 'en-US' });
// "One Hundred Twenty Three Trillion Four Hundred Fifty Six Billion..."

// Ordinals
toOrdinal(1, { localeCode: 'en-US' });  // "First"
toOrdinal(21, { localeCode: 'en-US' }); // "Twenty First"

// Indian numbering system
toWords(100000, { localeCode: 'en-IN' });   // "One Lakh"
toWords(10000000, { localeCode: 'en-IN' }); // "One Crore"
```

## Error Handling

```js
// Unknown locale throws
new ToWords({ localeCode: 'xx-XX' }); // Error: Unknown Locale "xx-XX"

// Non-finite numbers throw
toWords(Infinity);  // Error: Invalid Number
toWords(NaN);       // Error: Invalid Number

// Ordinal requires non-negative integer
toOrdinal(1.5);  // Error: Invalid Number. Number must be a non-negative integer
```
